category_title: Students who become bored and disruptive or sleepy during constant lecturing
minimal_context: The student is participating in an algebra class where the teacher is dictating notes for over 20 minutes.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please copy down the next set of algebra rules as I dictate them to you.

STUDENT: (Yawns) You just tell and tell and tell... we only dictate even in algebra.

TUTOR: Just keep writing what I am saying.

STUDENT: (Puts head on desk) It's been 20 minutes of just focusing on the course, I feel sleepy.

TUTOR: Sit up and focus on the algebra.

STUDENT: Hey, did anyone watch the game last night? I'm bored.

options.A: Provide a list of all the elements the student is supposed to perform during the algebra lesson.
options.B: Make an effort to keep the student's attention alive and encourage active participation in the algebra lesson.
options.C: Provide copies of the algebra notes that the tutor had dictated during the class instruction for the student.
options.D: Allow the student to withdraw from the algebra lesson for a time-limited period to prevent emotional overload.
